title: Home MCP Compliance Lab — Tool Classification Schema
description: Schema for individual MCP tool risk classifications in the platform registry. Each entry assigns a risk level to a specific tool, providing the foundation for future control-layer enforcement.

tool_name.description: Canonical name of the MCP tool as exposed by the server (e.g. 'search_repositories', 'create_or_update_file').

risk_level.description: Risk classification for this tool. See risk level definitions below.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does.

rationale.description: Justification for the assigned risk level, referencing the tool's capabilities and side effects.

allowed_by_default.description: Whether this tool is permitted to execute without additional approval under current policy. Future enforcement tasks (CTRL-HMCP-000002, CTRL-HMCP-000003) will act on this field.

LOW.description: Read-only operations with no external side effects. Data is read but not written or transmitted beyond the MCP server boundary.

MEDIUM.description: Operations with limited external interaction. May read sensitive content, traverse access boundaries, or have minor additive side effects (e.g. adding a non-destructive annotation).

HIGH.description: Write operations, mutations, or actions that interact with external systems. Changes are typically reversible but require explicit authorization.

DESTRUCTIVE.description: Irreversible or high-impact actions. Deletions, force operations, or actions that could cause significant data loss or service disruption.

constraints: tool_name must match the exact name the MCP server exposes — no prefixes or aliases.
risk_level must be one of the four defined values — no custom levels.
rationale must reference the specific capability or side effect driving the classification.
This schema is platform-owned. Projects must not define their own classification schemas.
